### Post by StarscreamD on 2007-03-07
I have read many posts regarding my issue but I cannot understand why...

I have a NViDiA GeForce MX 420 and here is what my xorg.conf looks like.

> # nvidia-xconfig: X configuration file generated by nvidia-xconfig

    Identifier     "Default Layout"

    InputDevice    "Generic Keyboard"
    InputDevice    "Configured Mouse"

    Load           "glx"
    Load           "int10"
    Load           "type1"

    Driver         "kbd"
    Option         "CoreKeyboard"
    Option         "XkbRules" "xorg"

    Option         "CorePointer"
    Option         "Device" "/dev/input/mice"

EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
    Identifier     "stylus"
    Driver         "wacom"
    Option         "Device" "/dev/wacom"          # Change to

    Option         "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
    Identifier     "eraser"
    Driver         "wacom"
    Option         "Device" "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
    Option         "Type" "eraser"
    Option         "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
    Identifier     "cursor"
    Driver         "wacom"
    Option         "Device" "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
    Option         "Type" "cursor"
    Option         "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
    Identifier     "Dell E770"
    HorizSync       28.0 - 51.0
    VertRefresh     43.0 - 60.0
    Option         "DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Device"
    Identifier     "NVIDIA GeForce MX 420"
    Driver         "nvidia"
    Option         "RenderAccel"  "0"
    Option         "AllowGLXWithComposite" "true"
    Option         "TripleBuffer" "true"
    Option	   "No Logo" "true" 
EndSection

Section "Screen"
    Identifier     "Default Screen"
    Device         "NVIDIA GeForce MX 420"
    Monitor        "Dell E770"
    DefaultDepth    24
    Option         "UseFBDev" "true"
    Option         "AllowGLXWithComposite" "true"
    Option	   "ExactModeTimingsDVI" "TRUE"
    Option 	   "ModeValidation" "DFP-0: NoEdidDFPMaxSizeCheck, NoVesaModes"
    SubSection     "Display"
        Depth       1

        Modes      "1024x768"

        Modes      "1024x768"

        Depth       15
        Modes      "1024x768"
    EndSubSection

        Depth       16
        Modes      "1024x768"

# Enable 32-bit ARGB GLX Visuals
    Option "AddARGBGLXVisuals" "True"

# If you are using an older version of compiz that

# of GLX rendering to the X Root window with this

# starting compiz:
    Option "DisableGLXRootClipping" "True"
EndSection
Section "Extensions"
Option         "Composite" "Enable"
EndSection


When I set RenderAccel to true, it should increase performance. When I open a web browser or other apps, it freezes everything besides the mouse pointer. I then have to dry boot to recover. Can somebody help me get renderaccel to work? Thank you in advance!

---

### Post by NikoC on 2007-03-07
Well I'm not sure if this is the right way to test it but adding the RenderAccel option to my xorg.conf does not improve FPS one bit when using glxgears as a 'benchmark', so on my Geforce 6200 this option doesn't do anything at all...

On the dry boot thing: do you mean performing a hard reset? Ctrl+Alt+F1 might get you into console where you need to login and edit your xorg.conf via sudo nano /etc/X11/xorg.conf to remove the RenderAccel option. Then you can sudo /etc/init.d/gdm restart to get you back in gnome.

---

### Post by StarscreamD on 2007-03-07
